text: hi everyone welcome to my channel this is a case of IPAM plus with atatiolysis the seventy three year old male who has no medical comorbidity presented with intermittent pain abdomen and swelling in for past two months this history of multiple surgeries which included a open sigmoidectomy with ileostomy and it was followed by an ileostomy closure. The present CT shows herniation of small bowel loop through the previous ileostomy closure site and few adhesive bands were noted in the CT scan.
The laparoscopic view shows the internal anatomy with the entry made through the Palmer's point, and two lateral ports are positioned to triangulate the surgical area.

The surgeon performs adhesiolysis by applying traction and counter traction to divide flimsy adhesions, creating working space. The omentum is carefully separated from the bowel, which is adhered to the abdominal wall.

The surgeon uses a 30-degree laparoscopic camera inserted through the Palmer's point port to visualize the adhesions from multiple angles. The camera is manipulated to carefully assess the adhesions before proceeding with their division, ensuring a slow and cautious approach to avoid bowel injury.

There is a concern and a lot of debates happen over the issue of enterotomy during such kind of aditiolysis. Though many publications are there now to support that if there is serosal tear or if there is minor enterotomy without significant spillage, a mesh can be kept but definitely no one would like to encounter such a situation and safety is of utmost importance while doing such type of cases.

The surgeon uses a closed grasper to apply traction on the bowel adhered to the anterior abdominal wall, gradually pulling it down. Sharp division is performed on flimsy adhesions, following the principle of traction and counter-traction to increase working space. The surgeon focuses on easier areas first to facilitate surgical progress before addressing more challenging adhesions.
There will be certain areas where the additions will be tough and I should be very cautious but certainly few bold steps needs to be taken for the progression of the surgery
The surgeon identifies the iliostomy site where the bowel has herniated. The herniated bowel loop is gradually drawn down from either side of the hernia area.

The surgeon performs division of the hernia sac, using traction and counter-traction techniques to separate the herniated bowel. The herniated bowel is visibly reduced, and the surgeon carefully dissects, taking some peritoneum and sheath along with the bowel.
A lupithalon suture is passed through the skin into the abdomen and threaded continuously across the hernia defect for closure. The suture is then retrieved from the entry site and tied externally.

A 15x15 cm composite mesh is placed inside the abdominal cavity for hernia repair. Upper and lower transfixation sutures are applied, followed by multiple 2-0 PDS sutures to secure the mesh, ensuring no gaps remain to prevent bowel herniation. The omentum is repositioned over the small bowel.
